関連科目: This is a high-level, English-only "content-based" course. Therefore, it is preferable for students to have already taken similar high-level courses like: Listening and Speaking 3, Critical Writing and thinking, language and Communication
授業方法: Students can expect to actively discuss and review course content through pair work or small groupsLessons will foster student autonomy and enhance students' ability to critically assess course content in an English-only environment.Students will NEED access to a computer at home (not just a smartphone) or use a school computer lab to complete and submit assignments properly. Desktop, laptop, notebook, Macbook, etc, are fine. Students should also have a Google account and sign up for Google Drive and Google Classroom (both are free).

トピック.授業計画1: Note: This syllabus may be subject to revisions according to student needs and abilities at the teacher's discretion
トピック.授業外学習2:
Students must have regular access to computers and the internet either at home or through an on-campus computer lab. Online, on-demand or simultaneous interactive tasks may be required.

The amount of study time for homework will vary from week to week and will depend on 1) the content being reviewed, and 2) the ability of individual students to understand the content to complete tasks. However, students can expect to do a minimum of 1 hours and a maximum of 2 hours of study each week.

教科書/Required texts0:
Students will need a Netflix account (Minimum access of 4 months either through pre-paid card or by credit card)

No textbook - Other materials provided by the teacher
参考文献・資料/Reference books0:
Electronic dictionary or equivalent, Notebooks

students will need a Google account (Gmail) and regular access to the Internet to access Google Classroom
学生による授業評価の方法Course Evaluation by Student: 各授業科目は、各学部・研究科の定めるディプロマ・ポリシー（DP）・カリキュラム・ポリシー（CP）に基づき、カリキュラム上に配置されています。DP・CPを意識して本授業科目の学修を進めることで、各学部・研究科の期待する能力が養成されます。各学部・研究科のDP、CPや教育課程表（授業科目一覧等）は、下記リンクこちらに掲載していますので学修計画の参考にしてください。https://kwic.kwansei.ac.jp/cabinet/reference?typeCd=0&cabinetId=557&directLink=1※kwicへのリンクとなります（在学生のみ閲覧可）

成績評価Grading1: 30% Course Notebook, 20% Review quizzes; 40% IN-CLASS Focused Discussions; 10% Flex Hours Assignments, There is no final examination(定期試験).

備考Note0:
この授業を履修するには、事前にレベル認定の申請が必要です。詳細は下記URL（kwicのキャビネット）の「履修手続」を確認してください。

If students are late to class, they are considered tardy. If more than 30 minutes late, they are considered absent. Two instances of tardiness are counted as one absence. One point per tardiness and two points per absence is taken off the final grade. A combination of 3 absences / 6 tardiness will result in a failing grade and the students expulsion from the class.
